text.title: Mom Test Interview Method and Agentic Workflow
text.summary: Mom Test question rules, bad-question antipatterns, the 3-agent agentic workflow for validation, and best practices for running and scoring interviews.

rule.statement: Ask only about the interviewee's past behavior and current life; never ask about your idea or their hypothetical future behavior.
rule.rationale: Questions about your idea trigger politeness bias. Questions about their past behavior reveal what they actually do, not what they wish they did.

list.item: Instead of "Would you use this?" → ask "How do you currently solve X?"
Instead of "Is this a problem for you?" → ask "Tell me about the last time you dealt with X."
Instead of "Would you pay $X?" → ask "What have you tried? What did it cost?"
Instead of "Do you think this is a good idea?" → ask "When was the last time you faced this problem?"

antipattern.description: LLM-generated interview scripts that re-introduce "would you" / "do you think" question forms.
antipattern.reason: Script-writer agents default to these polite forms because they appear in most interview templates. Lint every agent-generated script before use; reject any question with "would you" or "do you think".

rule.statement: Run at least 5 interviews before making any decision; run at least 10 before claiming confidence. Variance is too high below 5.
rule.rationale: A single enthusiastic interview is anecdote, not signal. Pattern recognition requires minimum sample size; 5 interviews gives themes, 10+ gives confidence.

rule.statement: Recruit cold (outside your network) for at least half of interviews; friends and warm contacts compliment.
rule.rationale: Network contacts give biased feedback. Cold recruits (LinkedIn DMs, Reddit, User Interviews) provide the unvarnished reality that network contacts suppress.

rule.statement: Record all interviews (with consent); memory of an interview is reconstructed and self-serving.
rule.rationale: Founders selectively remember the positive signals. Recording enables unbiased re-analysis and is required for agent-based transcript tagging.

section.title: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Three roles, three agents: (1) Script-prep agent (haiku): converts hypothesis into a Mom-Test-compliant interview guide using the vision/framing/weakness/pedestal/ask pattern. (2) Founder runs the actual interview — LLMs cannot replace this step. (3) Analysis agent (sonnet): ingests transcript, tags red flags (compliments, hypotheticals, generics), extracts commitment signals with severity, scores evidence on the 5-level hierarchy. (4) Decision agent (opus): aggregates at least 5 transcripts, recommends validated/inconclusive/invalidated with rationale and next-step plan. Human approves the final decision.

rule.statement: Never use a synthetic-user simulator as a substitute for real interviews; agent-to-agent "interviews" produce plausible but fabricated data.
rule.rationale: An LLM roleplay of a user produces responses consistent with the prompt framing, not responses consistent with actual user behavior. The resulting "validation" is confirmation bias at scale.

text.reference: Python script using Anthropic SDK to tag interview turns and return hierarchy-level scores
